identity.short_name: Count integer (a,b) with P(1)=0 and exactly one root in (0,1)

subdomain(s): polynomial real-root counting with parameter constraints
topic_path: parameter counting via convexity and anchored roots

problem_spec.summary: With integer parameters (a,b) in a box and constrained by P(1)=0, determine when a quintic has exactly one real root in (0,1) using a convex auxiliary function and a derivative-sign criterion, then count all lattice points satisfying the conditions.
ask: Count ordered pairs (a,b) satisfying the givens such that P(x)=0 has exactly one real root in (0,1).

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: controls the cubic-term contribution and (via H'(1)=a+1) the location of the convex minimum relative to x=1

parameter(s).name: b
parameter(s).purpose: controls the vertical shift of the auxiliary convex function H

parameter(s).name: anchored_root_point
parameter(s).purpose: imposes the affine constraint P(1)=0, i.e. forces the line b=-a-2 in the (a,b)-lattice

parameter(s).name: interval
parameter(s).purpose: interval in which the unique root is required

parameter(s).name: transform_choice
parameter(s).purpose: converts root counting for P on (0,∞) to convex analysis for H

invariant(s).name: strict_convexity
invariant(s).statement: H''(x)=6x+6/x^4>0 for x>0, so H is strictly convex on (0,∞).

invariant(s).name: anchored_value_at_one
invariant(s).statement: P(1)=a+b+2; the constraint P(1)=0 enforces H(1)=0.

invariant(s).name: derivative_at_one
invariant(s).statement: H'(1)=a+1, so the sign of a+1 determines whether the minimizer of H lies left or right of 1.

invariant(s).name: derivative_sign_criterion_for_root_left_of_one
invariant(s).statement: Under H(1)=0 and strict convexity, H has a (necessarily unique) root in (0,1) iff H'(1)>0.

essential_assumption(s): Consider x>0 when dividing by x^2.
Real analysis facts (strict convexity, monotonicity of H', IVT) apply on (0,∞).
The constraint P(1)=0 is enforced exactly in integers (a,b).
uniqueness_certificate(s): The condition P(1)=0 forces b=-a-2, reducing the parameter search to a finite set of integers a.
Strict convexity of H gives a rigid equivalence: existence of a root in (0,1) is determined solely by the sign of H'(1)=a+1.
Therefore the set of admissible (a,b) is uniquely determined by finitely many inequalities and has a unique count.

technique(s).name: convex_helper_function
technique(s).purpose: produce a strictly convex function whose zeros correspond to positive zeros of P

technique(s).name: anchored_root_reduction
technique(s).purpose: reduce the 2D lattice search to an affine line b=-a-2

technique(s).name: derivative_sign_classification_at_anchor
inputs: strict convexity of H and the anchored condition H(1)=0
technique(s).purpose: decide whether an additional root lies in (0,1) via the sign of H'(1)
outputs: criterion H has a root in (0,1) ⇔ H'(1)>0 ⇔ a\geq0 (for integer a)

theorem(s).name: Intermediate Value Theorem
theorem(s).role: certify existence of a root in (0,1) from a sign change between 0+ and the minimum point

theorem(s).name: Strict convexity implies monotone derivative and at most two zeros
theorem(s).role: guarantees uniqueness of the minimizer and bounds the number of positive roots

theorem(s).name: Convex anchored-zero derivative criterion
theorem(s).role: for strictly convex H with H(1)=0, a root in (0,1) exists iff H'(1)>0

theorem(s).name: basic lattice counting on a line segment in a box
theorem(s).role: count integer points satisfying b=-a-2 with bound constraints and a-inequality

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Convexity and monotonicity of derivatives control root existence; counting reduces to integer points on an affine constraint.

critical_trick(s): Divide by x^2 to obtain a strictly convex auxiliary function H on (0,∞).
Use the anchoring constraint P(1)=0 (so H(1)=0) and strict convexity to reduce root existence in (0,1) to the sign of H'(1)=a+1.
Count integer a satisfying the resulting inequalities and bounds for b=-a-2.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: optional exact verification of the (a,b) list by isolating real roots in (0,1)

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: optional brute-force enumeration over the finite lattice box for verification

task(s).name: brute_force_verify_count_under_anchor
task(s).reason: iterate all (a,b) in [-6,6]^2 with a+b=-2 and count real roots of P in (0,1) to confirm the analytic criterion
